generatedSentence: The diligent cyborg, a polished singer and drummer, spent weekends with his fiancée, often listening to violins. Their unity, born of shared origin, felt strong. He vaguely felt moody after a checkup, an unhappy variable. They were lovers, enjoying lychees by candlelight in their underground nest. His silver ukulele, always ready, offered sound, but he worried about a paranormal eclipse. One daylight, after a handshake with a novice babysitter, they took the elevator to inspect the rampart. They noticed fluffy black bear goujons and celery near a landmark, indicating customs were breached. A weird osmosis, kinda horny and jonesing, gave him malaria-like infection. Their goodwill was lost, sensing a worldwide monstrosity beyond their ecosystem. He watched a capybara roaming, hungry for onions, a dramedy unfolding. Later, a blackjack game turned sour when ninjas, craving revenge, whacked him, causing injury. He needed reinforcements but his signal was corked. The sound of fanjets meant ejection was near. A villein, whose meanings were unknown, brandished a javelin, ready to shatter their ideality. His earmuff and pyjamas were soaked as a griffon, with knots in its reins, injures his trustworthy nature. He gainsaid the sheriff, inoculating himself with valerian, kratom, and parsley, though he was lactose intolerant. His goal: escape this octopus of misery. With heels clicking, he found the pathway, avoiding a black bear. Daylight rising, he felt hopeful, beyond the natives' drachma, leaving the game. No more dope, no more mickey; he wouldn't linger, just kedging towards unity.
panelSentences: The diligent cyborg, a polished singer and drummer, spent weekends with his fiancée, often listening to violins. Their unity, born of shared origin, felt strong. He vaguely felt moody after a checkup, an unhappy variable. They were lovers, enjoying lychees by candlelight in their underground nest. His silver ukulele, always ready, offered sound, but he worried about a paranormal eclipse.

One daylight, after a handshake with a novice babysitter, they took the elevator to inspect the rampart. They noticed fluffy black bear goujons and celery near a landmark, indicating customs were breached. A weird osmosis, kinda horny and jonesing, gave him malaria-like infection. Their goodwill was lost, sensing a worldwide monstrosity beyond their ecosystem. He watched a capybara roaming, hungry for onions, a dramedy unfolding.

Later, a blackjack game turned sour when ninjas, craving revenge, whacked him, causing injury. He needed reinforcements but his signal was corked. The sound of fanjets meant ejection was near. A villein, whose meanings were unknown, brandished a javelin, ready to shatter their ideality. His earmuff and pyjamas were soaked as a griffon, with knots in its reins, injures his trustworthy nature.

He gainsaid the sheriff, inoculating himself with valerian, kratom, and parsley, though he was lactose intolerant. His goal: escape this octopus of misery. With heels clicking, he found the pathway, avoiding a black bear. Daylight rising, he felt hopeful, beyond the natives' drachma, leaving the game. No more dope, no more mickey; he wouldn't linger, just kedging towards unity.
rawPanelSentences: The diligent cyborg, a polished singer and drummer, spent weekends with his fiancée, often listening to violins. Their unity, born of shared origin, felt strong. He vaguely felt moody after a checkup, an unhappy variable. They were lovers, enjoying lychees by candlelight in their underground nest. His silver ukulele, always ready, offered sound, but he worried about a paranormal eclipse.

One daylight, after a handshake with a novice babysitter, they took the elevator to inspect the rampart. They noticed fluffy black bear goujons and celery near a landmark, indicating customs were breached. A weird osmosis, kinda horny and jonesing, gave him malaria-like infection. Their goodwill was lost, sensing a worldwide monstrosity beyond their ecosystem. He watched a capybara roaming, hungry for onions, a dramedy unfolding.

Later, a blackjack game turned sour when ninjas, craving revenge, whacked him, causing injury. He needed reinforcements but his signal was corked. The sound of fanjets meant ejection was near. A villein, whose meanings were unknown, brandished a javelin, ready to shatter their ideality. His earmuff and pyjamas were soaked as a griffon, with knots in its reins, injures his trustworthy nature.

He gainsaid the sheriff, inoculating himself with valerian, kratom, and parsley, though he was lactose intolerant. His goal: escape this octopus of misery. With heels clicking, he found the pathway, avoiding a black bear. Daylight rising, he felt hopeful, beyond the natives' drachma, leaving the game. No more dope, no more mickey; he wouldn't linger, just kedging towards unity.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a sleek silver cyborg with glowing blue optics, wearing a leather jacket and a single metallic arm. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A close-up of the cyborg (protagonist) and his fiancée holding hands, sharing lychees in a cozy, candlelit, underground nest, a polished ukulele resting nearby, soft lighting. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The cyborg standing on a futuristic rampart, looking down at a capybara foraging near a peculiar landmark, a subtle, eerie glow from a distant eclipse in the sky, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Action shot of the cyborg being 'whacked' by a ninja with a javelin in a dimly lit, intense scene, dynamic angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The cyborg, looking determined, scaling a rocky pathway towards a distant rising sun, leaving behind the dark, ominous landscape, triumphant low-angle.

definitions.definition: A small four-stringed guitar.
A mudcat (fish).
A thin strip of food, usually fish or chicken.
An American black bear, Ursus americanus, indigenous to North America.
An Asiatic black bear, Ursus thibetanus.

A musical four-string instrument, generally played with a bow or by plucking the string, with the pitch set by pressing the strings at the appropriate place with the fingers; also any instrument of the violin family.
A violinist.
definitions.example: The first violin often plays the lead melody lines in a string quartet.

definitions.definition: A person who is part machine, a robot who is part organic.
A robot who has an organic past.
A human with electronic or bionic prostheses.
A European herb (Apium graveolens) of the carrot family.
The stalks of this herb eaten as a vegetable.
A yellow-green colour, like that of a celery.
A routine visit to the doctor, dentist, or the like.
definitions.example: The appointment was just for a checkup.

definitions.definition: A routine inspection.
definitions.example: I took my car in for an annual checkup.

definitions.definition: To hit, slap or strike.
To kill, bump off.
To share or parcel out; often with up.
definitions.example: to whack the spoils of a robbery

definitions.definition: To beat convincingly; to thrash.
(usually in the negative) To surpass; to better.
Tired; fatigued.
definitions.example: After that all-night party, we were all whacked.

definitions.definition: Intoxicated on drugs.
A type of turret on the roof of certain medieval buildings designed to allow ventilation or the admission of light.
(chiefly in plural) A series of sloping overlapping slats or boards which admit air and light but exclude rain etc.
Any of a system of slits, as in the hood of an automobile, for ventilation.
One who loves and cares for another person in a romantic way; a sweetheart, love, soulmate, boyfriend, girlfriend or spouse.
A sexual partner, especially one with whom someone is having an affair.
A person who loves something.
definitions.example: a lover of fine wines

definitions.definition: (with "my") An informal term of address for any friend.
definitions.example: All right, me lover?

definitions.definition: The method of performance in any art; technique.
(in the plural) Technical terms or objects; things pertaining to the practice of an art or science.
(in the plural) The doctrine of arts in general; those branches of learning that relate to the arts.
The grasping of hands by two people when greeting, leave-taking, or making an agreement.
An exchange of signals between two devices when communications begin in order to ensure synchronization.
To perform a handshake; to shake hands.
To perform a handshake with another device.
Anything that raises or uplifts.
A permanent construction with a built-in platform that is lifted vertically, used to transport people and goods.
A silo used for storing wheat, corn or other grain (grain elevator)
A control surface of an aircraft responsible for controling the pitching motion of the machine.
A dental instrument used to pry up ("elevate") teeth in difficult extractions, or depressed portions of bone.
Any muscle that serves to raise a part of the body, such as the leg or the eye.
A type of shoe having an insert lift to make the wearer appear taller.
An alignment of astronomical objects whereby one object comes between the observer (or notional observer) and another object, thus obscuring the latter.

Especially, an alignment whereby a planetary object (for example, the Moon) comes between the Sun and another planetary object (for example, the Earth), resulting in a shadow being cast by the middle planetary object onto the other planetary object.
A seasonal state of plumage in some birds, notably ducks, adopted temporarily after the breeding season and characterised by a dull and scruffy appearance.
Obscurity, decline, downfall
Of astronomical bodies, to cause an eclipse.
definitions.example: The Moon eclipsed the Sun.

definitions.definition: To overshadow; to be better or more noticeable than.
(Irish grammar) To undergo eclipsis.
An underground railway, especially for mass transit of people in urban areas.
A train that runs on such an underground railway.
A rapid transit system, regardless of the elevation of its right of way.
An underground walkway, tunnel for pedestrians (called pedestrian underpass in US).
An underground route for pipes, sewers, etc.
Regions beneath the surface of the earth, both natural (eg. caves) and man-made (eg. mines).
(with definite article) A movement or organisation of people who resist political convention.
definitions.example: the French underground during World War II

definitions.definition: (with definite article) A movement or organisation of people who resist artistic convention.
To route electricity distribution cables underground
Below the ground; below the surface of the Earth.
definitions.example: There is an underground tunnel that takes you across the river.

definitions.definition: Hidden, furtive, secretive.
definitions.example: These criminals operate through an underground network.

definitions.definition: Outside the mainstream, especially unofficial and hidden from the authorities.
definitions.example: underground music

definitions.definition: Below the ground.
definitions.example: The tunnel goes underground at this point.

definitions.definition: Secretly.
To shine; to make a surface very smooth or shiny by rubbing, cleaning, or grinding.
definitions.example: He polished up the chrome until it gleamed.

definitions.definition: To refine; remove imperfections from.
definitions.example: The band has polished its performance since the last concert.

definitions.definition: To apply shoe polish to shoes.
To become smooth, as from friction; to receive a gloss; to take a smooth and glossy surface.
definitions.example: Steel polishes well.

definitions.definition: To refine; to wear off the rudeness, coarseness, or rusticity of; to make elegant and polite.
Made smooth or shiny by polishing.
definitions.example: polished shoes

definitions.definition: Refined, elegant.
definitions.example: a polished performance

definitions.definition: A common gambling card game in casinos, where the object is to get as close to 21 without going over.
A hand in the game of blackjack consisting of a face card and an ace.
The flag (i.e., a jack) traditionally flown by pirate ships; popularly thought to be a white skull and crossed bones on a black field (the Jolly Roger).

A small, flat, blunt, usually leather-covered weapon loaded with heavy material such as lead or ball bearings, intended to inflict a blow to the head that renders the victim unconscious with diminished risk of lasting cranial trauma.
Any of several species of weed of genus Bidens, such as Bidens pilosa, in the family Compositae.
A blackjack oak.
To strike with a blackjack or similar weapon.
A fragment of anything shattered.
definitions.example: to break a glass into shatters

definitions.definition: A (pine) needle.
A form of concentrated cannabis.
To violently break something into pieces.
definitions.example: The miners used dynamite to shatter rocks.

definitions.definition: To destroy or disable something.
To smash, or break into tiny pieces.
To dispirit or emotionally defeat
definitions.example: to be shattered in intellect; to have shattered hopes, or a shattered constitution

definitions.definition: To scatter about.
A person who sings, often professionally.
(square dance) dance figure with a fixed structure, sung by a caller, or a piece of music with that structure.
A hardy perennial flowering plant, Valeriana officinalis, with heads of sweetly scented pink or white flowers.
More generally, any plant of the genus Valeriana.
The root of Valeriana officinalis, used in herbal medicine.
An extract of the dried roots of the Valeriana officinalis used in herbal medicine as a sedative.
A semi-aquatic South American rodent, Hydrochoerus hydrochaeris, the largest living rodent.
An object that marks the boundary of a piece of land (usually a stone, or a tree).
A recognizable natural or man-made feature used for navigation.
A notable location with historical, cultural, or geographical significance.
A major event or discovery.
definitions.example: a landmark paper in neurosurgery

definitions.definition: To officially designate a site or building as a landmark.
The rear part of the foot, where it joins the leg.
The part of a shoe's sole which supports the foot's heel.
The rear part of a sock or similar covering for the foot.
The part of the palm of a hand closest to the wrist.
definitions.example: He drove the heel of his hand into the man's nose.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) A woman's high-heeled shoe.
The back, upper part of the stock.
The last or lowest part of anything.
definitions.example: the heel of a mast

definitions.definition: A crust end-piece of a loaf of bread.
The base of a bun sliced in half lengthwise.
A contemptible, inconsiderate or thoughtless person.
A headlining wrestler regarded as a "bad guy," whose ring persona embodies villainous or reprehensible traits and demonstrates characteristics of a braggart and a bully.
The cards set aside for later use in a patience or solitaire game.
Anything resembling a human heel in shape; a protuberance; a knob.
The lower end of a timber in a frame, as a post or rafter.
(specifically) The obtuse angle of the lower end of a rafter set sloping.
(workman slang) A cyma reversa.
The short side of an angled cut.
The part of a club head's face nearest the shaft.
The lower end of the bit (cutting edge) of an axehead; as opposed to the toe (upper end).
In a carding machine, the part of a flat nearest the cylinder.
The act of inclining or canting from a vertical position; a cant.
High-heeled shoes.
definitions.example: She wore a short skirt and heels.

definitions.definition: To examine critically or carefully; especially, to search out problems or determine condition; to scrutinize.
definitions.example: Inspect the system for leaks.

definitions.definition: To view and examine officially.
definitions.example: The general inspected the troops and their barracks.

definitions.definition: A mythical beast having the body of a lion and the wings and head of an eagle.
A large vulture (Gyps fulvus) found in the mountainous parts of Southern Europe, North Africa, and Asia Minor, supposed to be the "eagle" of the Bible.
An English variety of apple.
A person who has just arrived from Europe.
A cadet newly arrived in British India: half English, half Indian.
A watchful guardian, especially a duenna in charge of a young woman.
A breed of dog usually called the Brussels Griffon, reminiscent of the legendary creature.
A griffon vulture.
A favorably disposed attitude toward someone or something.
The value of a business entity not directly attributable to its tangible assets and liabilities. This value derives from factors such as consumer loyalty to the brand.

A concept used to refer to the ability of an individual or business to exert influence within a community, club, market or another type of group, without having to resort to the use of an asset (such as money or property), either directly or by the creation of a lien.
An adult male human.
definitions.example: The show is especially popular with middle-aged men.

definitions.definition: (collective) All human males collectively: mankind.
A human, a person of either gender, usually an adult. (See usage notes.)
definitions.example: every man for himself

definitions.definition: (collective) All humans collectively: mankind, humankind, humanity. (Sometimes capitalized as Man.)
A member of the genus Homo, especially of the species Homo sapiens.
An male person, usually an adult; a (generally adult male) sentient being, whether human, supernatural, elf, alien, etc.
An adult male who has, to an eminent degree, qualities considered masculine, such as strength, integrity, and devotion to family; a mensch.
Manliness; the quality or state of being manly.
A husband.
A lover; a boyfriend.
A male enthusiast or devotee; a male who is very fond of or devoted to a specified kind of thing. (Used as the last element of a compound.)
definitions.example: Some people prefer apple pie, but me, I’m a cherry pie man.

definitions.definition: A person, usually male, who has duties or skills associated with a specified thing. (Used as the last element of a compound.)
definitions.example: I wanted to be a guitar man on a road tour, but instead I’m a flag man on a road crew.

definitions.definition: A person, usually male, who can fulfill one's requirements with regard to a specified matter.
A male who belongs to a particular group: an employee, a student or alumnus, a representative, etc.
An adult male servant.
A vassal; a subject.
definitions.example: Like master, like man.

definitions.definition: A piece or token used in board games such as chess.
Used to refer to oneself or one's group: I, we; construed in the third person.
A term of familiar address often implying on the part of the speaker some degree of authority, impatience, or haste.
definitions.example: Come on, man, we've got no time to lose!

definitions.definition: A friendly term of address usually reserved for other adult males.
definitions.example: Hey, man, how's it goin'?

definitions.definition: A player on whom another is playing, with the intent of limiting their attacking impact.
A person trained in ninjutsu, especially one used for espionage, assassination, and other tasks requiring stealth during Japan's shogunate period.
A person considered similarly skillful to the historical ninja, especially in covert or stealthy operation.
A person considered to look like the historical ninja in some way, including (Mongolia) amateur private miners.
To act or move like a ninja, particularly with regard to a combination of speed, power, and stealth.
To claim an item through abuse of game mechanics.
definitions.example: That damn warrior ninja'd an epic-quality wand even though he can't even use it!

definitions.definition: To appropriate something (before someone else does).
To displace something, or take precedence over something.
To secure (land, etc.) by the right of preemption.
To make a preemptive bid at bridge.
(in the plural) The duties or taxes imposed on imported or exported goods.
definitions.example: Customs of £200 were due on all the wine we took back from France.

definitions.definition: (in the singular) The government department or agency that is authorised to collect the taxes imposed on imported goods.
definitions.example: Customs has pulled us over on our way for an inspection.

definitions.definition: Someone or something that has a fluffy texture.
A person who is superficial, who lacks depth or seriousness.
A babycino (frothy milk drink).
Covered with fluff.
Light; soft; airy.
Warm and comforting.
Not clearly defined or explained; fuzzy.
Lightweight; superficial; lacking depth or seriousness.
A looping of a piece of string or of any other long, flexible material that cannot be untangled without passing one or both ends of the material through its loops.
definitions.example: Climbers must make sure that all knots are both secure and of types that will not weaken the rope.

definitions.definition: (of hair, etc) A tangled clump.
definitions.example: The nurse was brushing knots from the protesting child's hair.

definitions.definition: A maze-like pattern.
A non-self-intersecting closed curve in (e.g., three-dimensional) space that is an abstraction of a knot (in sense 1 above).
definitions.example: A knot can be defined as a non-self-intersecting broken line whose endpoints coincide: when such a knot is constrained to lie in a plane, then it is simply a polygon.

definitions.definition: A difficult situation.
definitions.example: I got into a knot when I inadvertently insulted a policeman.

definitions.definition: The whorl left in lumber by the base of a branch growing out of the tree's trunk.
definitions.example: When preparing to tell stories at a campfire, I like to set aside a pile of pine logs with lots of knots, since they burn brighter and make dramatic pops and cracks.

definitions.definition: Local swelling in a tissue area, especially skin, often due to injury.
definitions.example: Jeremy had a knot on his head where he had bumped it on the bedframe.

definitions.definition: A protuberant joint in a plant.
Any knob, lump, swelling, or protuberance.
The swelling of the bulbus glandis in members of the dog family, Canidae
The point on which the action of a story depends; the gist of a matter.
definitions.example: the knot of the tale

definitions.definition: A node.
A kind of epaulet; a shoulder knot.
A group of people or things.
A bond of union; a connection; a tie.

A unit of speed, equal to one nautical mile per hour. (From the practice of counting the number of knots in the log-line (as it is paid out) in a standard time. Traditionally spaced at one every 1/120 of a mile.)
definitions.example: Cedric claimed his old yacht could make 12 knots.

definitions.definition: A nautical mile
The bulbus glandis
In omegaverse fiction, a bulbus glandis-like structure on the penis of a male alpha, which ties him to an omega during intercourse.
To form into a knot; to tie with a knot or knots.
definitions.example: We knotted the ends of the rope to keep it from unravelling.

definitions.definition: To form wrinkles in the forehead, as a sign of concentration, concern, surprise, etc.
definitions.example: She knotted her brow in concentration while attempting to unravel the tangled strands.

definitions.definition: To unite closely; to knit together.
To entangle or perplex; to puzzle.
To form knots.
To knit knots for a fringe.
One of a variety of shore birds; the red-breasted sandpiper (variously Calidris canutus or Tringa canutus).
Near the end of a period of time.
definitions.example: It was late in the evening when we finally arrived.

definitions.definition: Specifically, near the end of the day.
definitions.example: It was getting late and I was tired.

definitions.definition: (usually not comparable) Associated with the end of a period.
definitions.example: Late Latin is less fully inflected than classical Latin.

definitions.definition: Not arriving until after an expected time.
definitions.example: Even though we drove as fast as we could, we were still late.

definitions.definition: Not having had an expected menstrual period.
definitions.example: I'm late, honey. Could you buy a test?

definitions.definition: Deceased, dead: used particularly when speaking of the dead person's actions while alive. (Often used with "the"; see usage notes.)
definitions.example: Her late husband had left her well provided for.

definitions.definition: Existing or holding some position not long ago, but not now; departed, or gone out of office.
definitions.example: the late administration

definitions.definition: Recent — relative to the noun it modifies.
Of a star or class of stars, cooler than the sun.
After a deadline has passed, past a designated time.
definitions.example: We drove as fast as we could, but we still arrived late.

definitions.definition: Formerly, especially in the context of service in a military unit.
definitions.example: Colonel Easterwood, late of the 34th Carbines, was a guest at the dinner party.

definitions.definition: Not long ago; just now.
Afterward in time (used with than when comparing with another time).
definitions.example: I arrived later than my roommate.

definitions.definition: At some unspecified time in the future.
definitions.example: I wanted to do it now, but I’ll have to do it later.

definitions.definition: See you later; goodbye.
definitions.example: Later, dude.

definitions.definition: The act or process of infecting.
An uncontrolled growth of harmful microorganisms in a host.
A monocotyledonous plant (Allium cepa), allied to garlic, used as vegetable and spice.
The bulb of such a plant.
The genus as a whole.
A ball.
A person from Bermuda or of Bermudian descent.
Clothes for wearing to bed and sleeping in, usually consisting of a loose-fitting shirt and pants/trousers.
Loose-fitting trousers worn by both sexes in various southern Asian countries including India.
A person who cares for one or more babies or children for a short period of time in place of their legal guardians.
definitions.example: My neighbor is sometimes a babysitter for other moms' children.

definitions.definition: (except Scotland) (High Sheriff) An official of a shire or county office, responsible for carrying out court orders, law enforcement and other duties.
A judge in the sheriff court, the court of a county or sheriffdom.
A government official, usually responsible for law enforcement in his county and for administration of the county jail, sometimes an officer of the court, usually elected.
To carry out the duties of a sheriff
To wander or travel freely and with no specific destination.
To use a network or service from different locations or devices.
To transmit (resources) between different locations or devices, to allow comparable usage from any of them.
To range or wander over.
definitions.example: Gangs of thugs roamed the streets.

definitions.definition: An instance of wandering.
The ability to use a cell phone outside of its original registering zone.
The use of a network or service from different locations or devices.
(operating system) Using the OS service of manipulating folders and documents from different PC devices by a registered user.
The act, process, or state of reinforcing or being reinforced.
A thing that reinforces.
(in the plural) Additional troops or materiel sent to support a military action.
The process whereby a behavior with desirable consequences comes to be repeated.
Damage to the body of a human or animal.
definitions.example: The passenger sustained a severe injury in the car accident.

definitions.definition: The violation of a person's reputation, rights, property, or interests.
definitions.example: Slander is an injury to the character.

definitions.definition: Injustice.
To wrong, to injure.
To wound or cause physical harm to a living creature.
To damage or impair.
To do injustice to.
The net movement of solvent molecules, usually water, from a region of lower solute concentration to a region of higher solute concentration through a partially permeable membrane.
Picking up knowledge accidentally, without actually seeking that particular knowledge.
definitions.example: I was reading about chickens, and I guess I learned about hawks through osmosis.

definitions.definition: Healthy.
definitions.example: He was safe and sound.

definitions.definition: Complete, solid, or secure.
definitions.example: Fred assured me the floorboards were sound.

definitions.definition: Having the property of soundness.
Good; acceptable; decent.
definitions.example: "How are you?" - "I'm sound."

definitions.definition: (of sleep) Quiet and deep. Sound asleep means sleeping peacefully, often deeply.
definitions.example: Her sleep was sound.

definitions.definition: Heavy; laid on with force.
definitions.example: a sound beating

definitions.definition: Founded in law; legal; valid; not defective.
definitions.example: a sound title to land

definitions.definition: Soundly.
Yes; used to show agreement or understanding, generally without much enthusiasm.
One who plays the drums.
Travelling salesman
A drumstick (the lower part of a chicken or turkey leg).
Any of various fish of the family Kyphosidae which make a drumming sound.
The light from the Sun, as opposed to that from any other source.
A light source that simulates daylight.
(photometry) The intensity distribution of light over the visible spectrum generated by the Sun under various conditions or by other light sources intended to simulate natural daylight.
The period of time between sunrise and sunset.
definitions.example: We should get home while it's still daylight.

definitions.definition: Daybreak.
definitions.example: We had only two hours to work before daylight.

definitions.definition: Exposure to public scrutiny.
definitions.example: Budgeting a spy organization can't very well be done in daylight.

definitions.definition: A clear, open space.
definitions.example: All small running backs instinctively run to daylight.

definitions.definition: (machinery) The space between platens on a press or similar machinery.
definitions.example: The minimum and maximum daylights on an injection molding machine determines the sizes of the items it can make.

definitions.definition: Emotional or psychological distance between people, or disagreement.
definitions.example: We completely agree. There's no daylight between us on the issue.

definitions.definition: To expose to daylight
To provide sources of natural illumination such as skylights or windows.
To allow light in, as by opening drapes.
(landscaping) To run a drainage pipe to an opening from which its contents can drain away naturally.
To gain exposure to the open.
definitions.example: The seam of coal daylighted at a cliff by the river.

definitions.definition: A turbofan engine.
An airplane powered by turbofan engines.
To cause (something) to cease to be in one's possession or capability due to unfortunate or unknown circumstances, events or reasons.
definitions.example: He lost his hearing in the explosion.

definitions.definition: To wander from; to miss, so as not to be able to find; to go astray from.
definitions.example: I lost my way in the forest.

definitions.definition: To fail to win (a game, competition, trial, etc).
definitions.example: We lost the football match.

definitions.definition: To be unable to follow or trace (somebody or something) any longer.
definitions.example: Mission control lost the satellite as its signal died down.

definitions.definition: To cause (somebody) to be unable to follow or trace one any longer.
definitions.example: We managed to lose our pursuers in the forest.

definitions.definition: To cease exhibiting; to overcome (a behavior or emotion).
To shed, remove, discard, or eliminate.
definitions.example: When we get into the building, please lose the hat.

definitions.definition: Of a clock, to run slower than expected.
definitions.example: It's already 5:30? My watch must have lost a few minutes.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To cause (someone) the loss of something; to deprive of.
To fail to catch with the mind or senses; to miss.
definitions.example: I lost a part of what he said.

definitions.definition: To cause to part with; to deprive of.
Having wandered from, or unable to find, the way.
definitions.example: The children were soon lost in the forest.

definitions.definition: In an unknown location; unable to be found.
definitions.example: Deep beneath the ocean, the Titanic was lost to the world.

definitions.definition: Not perceptible to the senses; no longer visible.
definitions.example: an island lost in a fog; a person lost in a crowd

definitions.definition: Parted with; no longer held or possessed.
definitions.example: a lost limb; lost honour

definitions.definition: Not employed or enjoyed; thrown away; employed ineffectually; wasted; squandered.
definitions.example: a lost day; a lost opportunity or benefit

definitions.definition: Ruined or destroyed, either physically or morally; past help or hope.
definitions.example: a ship lost at sea; a woman lost to virtue; a lost soul

definitions.definition: Hardened beyond sensibility or recovery; alienated; insensible.
definitions.example: lost to shame; lost to all sense of honour

definitions.definition: Occupied with, or under the influence of, something, so as not to notice external things.
definitions.example: to be lost in thought

definitions.definition: Affected by hunger; desiring of food; having a physical need for food.
definitions.example: My kids go to bed hungry every night because I haven't got any money.

definitions.definition: Causing hunger
Eager, having an avid desire (‘appetite’) for something.
Not rich or fertile; poor; barren; starved.
definitions.example: a hungry soil

definitions.definition: A disease spread by mosquito, in which a protozoan, Plasmodium, multiplies in blood every few days.
Supposed poisonous air arising from marshy districts, once thought to cause fever.
A bright green, biennial herb, Petroselinum crispum, having many cultivars.
The leaves of this plant used in Middle Eastern, European, and American cooking.
A lustrous, white, metallic element, atomic number 47, atomic weight 107.87, symbol Ag.
(collectively) Coins made from silver or any similar white metal.
(collectively) Cutlery and other eating utensils, whether silver or made from some other white metal.
(collectively) Any items made from silver or any other white metal.
A shiny gray color.
Anything resembling silver; something shiny and white.
To acquire a silvery colour.
To cover with silver, or with a silvery metal.
definitions.example: to silver a pin;  to silver a glass mirror plate with an amalgam of tin and mercury

definitions.definition: To polish like silver; to impart a brightness to, like that of silver.
To make hoary, or white, like silver.
Made from silver.
Made from another white metal.
Having a color like silver: a shiny gray.
Denoting the twenty-fifth anniversary, especially of a wedding.
(of commercial services) Premium, but inferior to gold.
Having the clear, musical tone of silver; soft and clear in sound.
definitions.example: a silver-voiced young girl

definitions.definition: To warp (a vessel) by carrying out a kedge in a boat, dropping it overboard, and hauling the vessel up to it.
(of a vessel) To move with the help of a kedge, as described above.
The disaccharide sugar of milk and dairy products, C12H22O11, a product of glucose and galactose used as a food and in medicinal compounds.
Any of several marine molluscs of the family Octopodidae, having no internal or external protective shell or bone (unlike the nautilus, squid and cuttlefish) and eight arms each covered with suckers.
The flesh of these marine molluscs eaten as food.
An organization that has many powerful branches controlled from the centre.
To put (or attempt to put) one's fingers, hands or arms in many things or places at roughly the same time.
To spread out in long arms or legs in many directions.
To plug a large number of devices into a single electric outlet.
(by extension) To grow in use vastly beyond what was originally intended.
To hunt and catch octopuses.
To behave like an octopus.
To say something in contradiction to.
In a vague manner.
definitions.example: He looked vaguely around the room, as if lost.

definitions.definition: Loosely; more or less; somewhat.
A woman who is engaged to be married.
The break in the working week, usually two days including the traditional holy or sabbath day. Thus in western countries, Saturday and Sunday.
To spend the weekend.
definitions.example: We'll weekend at the beach.

definitions.definition: At weekends.
A tree, Mitragyna speciosa, endemic to Southeast Asia.
The dried leaves of this tree, used in traditional medicine or recreationally for their stimulant and analgesic effects.
Kind of; somewhat
definitions.example: I kinda hafta do this right now.

definitions.definition: Yes in some respects but no in other respects.
definitions.example: "Are you afraid of a little bit of rain?" "Kinda, yeah."

definitions.definition: Hard or bony, like an animal's horn.
Having horns.
Sexually aroused.
definitions.example: That girl makes me feel horny.

definitions.definition: Sexually arousing.
Oneness; the state or fact of being one undivided entity.
Agreement; harmony.
A single undivided thing, seen as complete in itself.

Any of the three classical rules of drama: unity of action (nothing should be admitted not directly relevant to the development of the plot), unity of place (the scenes should be set in the same place), and unity of time (all the events should be such as might happen within a single day).
The number 1 or any element of a set or field that behaves under a given operation as the number 1 behaves under multiplication.
The peculiar characteristics of an estate held by several in joint tenancy.

The form of consensus in a Quaker meeting for business which signals that a decision has been reached. In order to achieve unity, everyone who does not agree with the decision must explicitly stand aside, possibly being recorded in the minutes as doing so.
The beginning of something.
The source of a river, information, goods, etc.
The point at which the axes of a coordinate system intersect.
The proximal end of attachment of a muscle to a bone that will not be moved by the action of that muscle.
An arbitrary point on Earth's surface, chosen as the zero for a system of coordinates.
(in the plural) Ancestry.
To buy excessively, especially to buy more than one needs or can afford
To buy at an inflated price
A playful or competitive activity.
A video game.
(nearly always singular) A field of gainful activity, as an industry or profession.
definitions.example: He's in the securities game somehow.

definitions.definition: Something that resembles a game with rules, despite not being designed.
definitions.example: In the game of life, you may find yourself playing the waiting game far too often.

definitions.definition: An exercise simulating warfare, whether computerized or involving human participants.
Wild animals hunted for food.

definitions.definition: (used mostly of males) The ability to seduce someone, usually by strategy.

definitions.definition: Mastery; the ability to excel at something.
A questionable or unethical practice in pursuit of a goal; a scheme.
definitions.example: You want to borrow my credit card for a week? What's your game?

definitions.definition: To gamble.
To play card games, board games, or video games.
To exploit loopholes in a system or bureaucracy in a way which defeats or nullifies the spirit of the rules in effect, usually to obtain a result which otherwise would be unobtainable.

definitions.definition: (of males) To perform premeditated seduction strategy.
Willing to participate.
(of an animal) That shows a tendency to continue to fight against another animal, despite being wounded, often severely.
Persistent, especially in senses similar to the above.
Injured, lame (of a limb).
Given to sudden or frequent changes of mind; temperamental.
Sulky or depressed.
Dour, gloomy or brooding.
Dodgy or stolen.
Have an intense craving.

definitions.definition: Suffering from withdrawal, or otherwise being in desperate, addiction-like need of a substance.
A beginner; one who is not very familiar or experienced in a particular subject.

definitions.definition: A new member of a religious order accepted on a conditional basis, prior to confirmation.
Something that is variable.
Something whose value may be dictated or discovered.

definitions.definition: A quantity that may assume any one of a set of values.
A symbol representing a variable.
A named memory location in which a program can store intermediate results and from which it can read them.
A variable star.
A shifting wind, or one that varies in force.
(in the plural) Those parts of the sea where a steady wind is not expected, especially the parts between the trade-wind belts.
Able to vary or be varied.

definitions.definition: Likely to vary.
Marked by diversity or difference.
Having no fixed quantitative value.
Tending to deviate from a normal or recognized type.
An edible plant (Brassica oleracea var. capitata) having a head of green leaves.
The leaves of this plant eaten as a vegetable.

definitions.definition: A person with severely reduced mental capacities due to brain damage.

definitions.definition: Used as a term of endearment.
Money.
Marijuana leaf, the part that is not smoked but from which cannabutter can be extracted.
The terminal bud of certain palm trees, used for food.
The cabbage palmetto (Sabal palmetto), a palm of the southeastern US coasts and nearby islands.
To form a head like that of the cabbage.

definitions.definition: To do nothing; to idle; veg out.
A genre of film or television that lies somewhere between drama and comedy.
A film or television programme belonging to this genre.
Performing with industrious concentration; hard-working and focused.

definitions.definition: The act of ejecting.
That which is ejected.
The quality or state of being ideal.
The capacity to form deals of beauty or perfection.
The conceptive faculty.
An individual who is not happy.
Not happy; sad.
Not satisfied; unsatisfied.

definitions.definition: Not lucky; unlucky.

definitions.definition: Not suitable; unsuitable.
(of words or symbols) The entity, perception, feeling or concept thereby represented or evoked.
The value, purpose, importance, point or significance (of something beyond the fact of that thing's existence).

definitions.definition: The object or concept that a word or phrase denotes, or that which a sentence says.
Intention.
A small bottle of liquor, holding 375 ml or 13 oz., typically shaped to fit in one's pocket.
A Mickey Finn; a beverage, usually alcoholic, that has been drugged.
American depression era term for a potato as in a "roasted mickey".
The penis.
The vagina.
A well-known honeyeater, the Noisy Miner, Manorina melanocephala, of eastern Australia.

Piss, shortened and more commonly used form of Mickey Bliss.
The resolution of a mouse, used as a unit of length.
To secretly slip drugs into somebody's drink.
A light spear thrown with the hand and used as a weapon.
A metal-tipped spear thrown for distance in an athletic field event.
To pierce with a javelin.
A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.
A place used by another mammal, fish, amphibian or insect, for depositing eggs and hatching young.
A snug, comfortable, or cosy residence or job situation.
A retreat, or place of habitual resort.
A hideout for bad people to frequent or haunt; a den.

definitions.definition: A home that a child or young adult shares with a parent or guardian.

definitions.definition: A fixed number of cards in some bidding games awarded to the highest bidder allowing him to exchange any or all with cards in his hand.

definitions.definition: A fortified position for a weapon.
A structure consisting of nested structures, such as nested loops or nested subroutine calls.
A circular bed of pasta, rice, etc. to be topped or filled with other foods.

A compact group of pulleys, gears, springs, etc., working together or collectively.

To settle into a home.

definitions.definition: To successively neatly fit inside another.

definitions.definition: To place in, or as if in, a nest.
To place one thing neatly inside another, and both inside yet another (and so on).

definitions.definition: To hunt for birds' nests or their contents (usually "go nesting").
A feudal tenant.
To seal or stop up, especially with a cork stopper.
To blacken (as) with a burnt cork
To leave the cork in a bottle after attempting to uncork it.
To fill with cork, as the center of a baseball bat.

definitions.definition: To injure through a blow; to induce a haematoma.

To perform such a maneuver.
Of a container, especially a bottle, closed with a cork.

definitions.definition: Blackened by burnt cork.

definitions.definition: The light emitted by a candle.
Any viscous liquid or paste, such as a lubricant, used in preparing a surface.
An absorbent material used to hold a liquid.

Any illicit or narcotic drug that produces euphoria or satisfies an addiction; particularly heroin.
Information, usually from an inside source, originally in horse racing and other sports.

A stupid person.
Dessert topping.
To affect with drugs.

Amazing.

definitions.definition: To stay or remain in a place or situation, especially as if unwilling to depart or not easily able to do so; to loiter.
To remain alive or existent although still proceeding toward death or extinction; to die gradually.
(often followed by on) To consider or contemplate for a period of time; to engage in analytic thinking or discussion.
Deserving of trust, reliable.
Any form of personal retaliatory action against an individual, institution, or group for some alleged or perceived harm or injustice.

definitions.definition: A win by a previous loser.
To take revenge for (a particular harmful action) or on behalf of (its victim); to avenge.

definitions.definition: To take one's revenge (on or upon someone).
To take vengeance; to revenge itself.
A garment to keep the ears warm.
A garment or part worn over a single ear.
A sound-deadening cup or a pair of such cups worn over the ear or ears.

Objects designed to cover a person's ears for protection against cold or noise. They consist of a thermoplastic or metal head-band, that fits over the top of the head, and a pad at each end, to cover the external ears.
A system formed by an ecological community and its environment that functions as a unit.
The interconnectedness of organisms (plants, animals, microbes) with each other and their environment.
A set of interconnected products and services.
A result that one is attempting to achieve.

The act of placing the object into the goal.
A point scored in a game as a result of placing the object into the goal.

To score a goal.
An organism showing abnormal development or deformity.
A monstrous thing, person or act.
The state of being monstrous.
A person to whom paranormal powers are attributed.
(with "the") Paranormal phenomena collectively, or as a field of study.
That (ostensibly) cannot be explained by what scientists know; supernatural; especially of or pertaining to ghosts, spirits, and hypothesized abilities like telepathy.
A footpath or other path or track.
A sequence of biochemical compounds, and the reactions linking them, that describe a process in metabolism or catabolism.
A course of action.
The unknown.
The hereafter.
Something that is far beyond.
Farther along or away.
In addition; more.
Extremely, more than

definitions.definition: Further away than.

definitions.definition: On the far side of.

definitions.definition: Later than; after.
Greater than; so as to exceed or surpass.

definitions.definition: In addition to.
Past, or out of reach of.

definitions.definition: Not within the comprehension of.

A native speaker.
Ostrea edulis, a kind of oyster.
The currency of Greece in ancient times and again from 1832 until 2001, with the symbol ₯, since replaced by the euro.

A later Greek weight equal to a gram.
Spanning the world; global.

definitions.definition: Throughout the world.

That tree's bright red oval fruit with a single stone surrounded by a fleshy white aril.
A soft pink-red colour, like that of a lychee rind (also called lychee red).
At all times; throughout all time; since the beginning.
definitions.example: Airplanes did not always exist as a form of transportation.

definitions.definition: Constantly during a certain period, or regularly at stated intervals (opposed to sometimes or occasionally).
definitions.example: In this street, the shops always close during lunchtime.

definitions.definition: In any event.

To come; to offer itself.

Rebellion.
The act of something that rises.

More than; exceeding; upwards of.

A defensive structure; a protective barrier; a bulwark.
That which defends against intrusion from outside; a protection.
(usually in the plural) A steep bank of a river or gorge.
To defend with a rampart; fortify or surround with a rampart.
Someone who is hoping for success or victory, especially as a candidate in a political election.
definitions.example: Several presidential hopefuls are campaigning in New Hampshire this week.

definitions.definition: Feeling hope

definitions.definition: Inspiring hope
To introduce an antigenic substance or vaccine into something (e.g. the body) or someone, such as to produce immunity to a specific disease.
(by extension) To safeguard or protect something as if by inoculation.
